title: Phegopteris simplex
intro: Phegopteris simplex, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. Traditionally, Phegopteris simplex has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phegopteris simplex is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in the form of infusions or tinctures, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
